questions.q: Hanuman is the son of which god?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is the son of Vayu, the wind-god, which is why he is called the Son of the Wind.

questions.q: To which race of beings does Hanuman belong?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is a vanara, one of the monkey-like forest dwellers of Kishkindha.

questions.q: Whom does Hanuman serve as a supreme devotee in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is celebrated as the supreme devotee of Rama, serving him with unwavering loyalty.

questions.q: Hanuman leaps across the ocean to reach which place?
questions.explanation: Hanuman makes his famous leap across the ocean to the island of Lanka in search of Sita.

questions.q: Whom does Hanuman search for and find in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman crosses to Lanka to find Sita, whom Ravana had abducted, and discovers her in the Ashoka grove.

questions.q: Where in Lanka does Hanuman find Sita held captive?
questions.explanation: Hanuman finds Sita guarded by rakshasis in the Ashoka grove of Ravana's kingdom.

questions.q: What token does Rama give Hanuman to show Sita as proof of his identity?
questions.explanation: Rama gives Hanuman his signet ring, which Hanuman shows to Sita so she will trust him as Rama's messenger.

questions.q: What is one of Hanuman's most famous abilities?
options: Changing the size of his body at will
questions.explanation: Hanuman can change his size at will, growing colossal to leap the ocean or shrinking small to enter Lanka unseen.

questions.q: Which weapon is most commonly associated with Hanuman?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is traditionally depicted carrying a mace (gada), the emblem of his great strength.

questions.q: How does Hanuman leave a dramatic mark on Lanka before departing?
options: He sets the city on fire with his burning tail
questions.explanation: After the rakshasas set his tail ablaze, Hanuman uses the fire to burn much of Lanka before leaping away.

questions.q: Who was Hanuman's mother?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's mother was Anjana, an apsara-born vanara woman, which gives him the name Anjaneya.

questions.q: Where does Hanuman first meet Rama and Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, serving Sugriva, first meets Rama and Lakshmana near the Rishyamukha mountain by Pampa lake.

questions.q: Whose minister and loyal companion is Hanuman among the vanaras?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is the trusted minister and counselor of Sugriva, the exiled and later restored vanara king.

questions.q: Which mountain did Hanuman press down with his foot so he could spring across the ocean?
questions.explanation: Hanuman launched his great leap to Lanka from Mount Mahendra, pressing it down as he sprang.

questions.q: Which sea-serpent demoness tried to swallow Hanuman during his ocean crossing, whom he outwitted by shrinking?
questions.explanation: Surasa, mother of serpents, tested Hanuman by trying to swallow him, but he shrank to thumb-size, entered and left her mouth, and passed.

questions.q: Which rakshasi did Hanuman defeat upon first reaching Lanka's shore, fulfilling an omen that the city's fall was near?
questions.explanation: Hanuman struck down Lankini, the guardian spirit of the city, whose defeat was an omen that Lanka would fall.

questions.q: When Hanuman was captured in Lanka, with what was his tail set ablaze?
questions.explanation: On Ravana's order, the rakshasas wrapped Hanuman's tail in oil-soaked cloth and set it on fire.

questions.q: How was Hanuman brought before Ravana in the assembly hall after his capture?
options: He was bound by Indrajit's Brahmastra
questions.explanation: Indrajit, Ravana's son, bound Hanuman with the Brahmastra; Hanuman allowed the binding to hold so he could meet Ravana.

questions.q: What did Sita give Hanuman to carry back to Rama as a sign that he had truly found her?
questions.explanation: Sita gave Hanuman her chudamani (a jeweled hair ornament) as a token for Rama, proving he had found her.

questions.q: During the war, Hanuman fetched an entire mountain to save Lakshmana's life. What healing herb was needed?
questions.explanation: Hanuman flew to the Himalayas for the Sanjivani herb and, unable to identify it, carried back the whole mountain.

questions.q: Why did Hanuman bring back the entire mountain rather than just the herb?
options: The mountain was sacred and could not be cut
He could not identify which herb was the right one
The herb only worked while rooted
questions.explanation: Unable to tell which glowing herb was Sanjivani, Hanuman uprooted the whole mountain and carried it to the battlefield.

questions.q: Which elder vanara reminded Hanuman of his forgotten powers, inspiring him to leap the ocean?
questions.explanation: Jambavan, the wise bear-king, reminded Hanuman of his immense strength and powers, rousing him to make the great leap.

questions.q: As a child, Hanuman leapt toward the sky to seize the sun, mistaking it for a fruit. Which god struck him with a weapon to stop him?
questions.explanation: Indra struck the child Hanuman with his vajra (thunderbolt) on the jaw to stop him from seizing the sun, which is the origin of the name Hanuman.

questions.q: The name 'Hanuman' is traditionally derived from an injury to which part of his body?
questions.explanation: The name Hanuman comes from 'hanu' (jaw), referring to the jaw injured by Indra's thunderbolt in his childhood.

questions.q: Because of a curse pronounced by sages, Hanuman remained unaware of his own great powers until reminded. What was the nature of this curse?
options: He would forget his strength until someone recounted it to him
He would lose his powers in sunlight
He could never speak of his deeds
questions.explanation: Sages cursed the mischievous young Hanuman to forget his powers until reminded of them, which is why Jambavan later had to rouse his memory.

questions.q: Which son of Ravana did Hanuman kill in battle while destroying the grove and provoking the rakshasas?
questions.explanation: Hanuman slew Aksha, the young son of Ravana, before Indrajit was sent to capture him.

questions.q: Whom did Hanuman carry on his shoulders during the war so that the warrior could fight Ravana's forces, as Rama rode Hanuman?
questions.explanation: In the battle, Rama mounted Hanuman's shoulders, while Lakshmana rode Angada, as the vanaras served as the brothers' mounts.

questions.q: On his journey to Lanka, Hanuman rested on a mountain that rose from the sea to offer him hospitality. What was its name?
questions.explanation: The golden mountain Mainaka rose from the ocean and offered Hanuman rest, but he touched it in thanks and flew onward without stopping.

questions.q: Which kind rakshasi comforted Sita in the Ashoka grove and described favorable dreams foretelling Rama's victory?
questions.explanation: Trijata, a compassionate rakshasi, reassured Sita by recounting auspicious dreams that foretold Ravana's defeat and Rama's victory.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, Hanuman is described as deeply learned, said to have mastered the scriptures by studying with which celestial body as his teacher?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is said to have learned the Vedas and grammar from Surya, the sun, running alongside the sun's chariot to receive instruction.
